pr.title: Fix non-matching that was caused by externs
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Removed an inline assembly fallback and NON_MATCHING guard around HSD_RenderInitAllocData in src/sysdolphin/baselib/tev.c, leaving the C implementation as the active matching version. The PR title and removed comment indicate the prior mismatch was caused by extern handling and was fixed once those extern-related layout/splitting issues were resolved.
what_changed: Deleted the comment stating that HSD_RenderInitAllocData was not matching because of externs and would match once split properly.
Removed the #ifdef NON_MATCHING / #else / #endif wrapper around HSD_RenderInitAllocData.
Deleted the hand-written asm implementation of HSD_RenderInitAllocData.
Kept the C implementation that calls HSD_ObjAllocInit for render_alloc_data, tevreg_alloc_data, and chan_alloc_data with sizes 0x1C, 0x14, and 0x30 and alignment/count argument 4.
smart_moves: Once the extern-related mismatch was fixed, the PR immediately removed the asm fallback instead of leaving stale NON_MATCHING scaffolding.
The C function expresses the same sequence as the deleted assembly more maintainably: three HSD_ObjAllocInit calls over the render, TEV register, and channel allocation data objects.
The change is intentionally narrow: one file, one function, no unrelated cleanup.
decomp_lessons: Extern declarations and data splitting can affect whether nearby C code matches; a non-match may be caused by symbol placement or data layout rather than incorrect function logic.
When an asm fallback only exists because of unresolved extern/data layout issues, revisit it after splitting or symbol placement work and remove the NON_MATCHING guard if the C now matches.
For allocator initialization functions, verify that compiler output may depend on the contiguous layout or ordering of allocation-data globals referenced through externs.
naming_conventions: HSD_RenderInitAllocData uses the HSD subsystem prefix and describes initialization of render allocation data.
HSD_RenderGetAllocData follows the same HSD_Render*AllocData naming family visible adjacent to the changed function.
Allocation data globals use snake_case names ending in _alloc_data, such as render_alloc_data, tevreg_alloc_data, and chan_alloc_data.
Unidentified or still-extern labels retain lbl_804D7604/lbl_804D760C style names until better names are available.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed inline asm after the C version matched, reducing reliance on asm blocks.

The deleted asm showed the compiler previously addressed render_alloc_data, then used offsets 0x2C and 0x58 for subsequent allocator data; the C version uses named globals instead, relying on correct data declarations/splitting to produce the same addressing.
The matching tactic was not to alter function logic, but to fix extern/data layout issues so the straightforward C implementation could match.
searchable_terms: Fix non-matching that was caused by externs
follow_up_queries: Find earlier or adjacent changes that split or declared render_alloc_data, tevreg_alloc_data, and chan_alloc_data correctly.
Search for other NON_MATCHING blocks whose comments blame externs or unsplit data.
Audit sysdolphin/baselib allocation-data globals for contiguous-layout assumptions similar to the deleted asm offsets 0x2C and 0x58.
